questions.scenario: A legacy invoice worker expects a mounted folder path and several workers may need to read the same small files during a migration. The team does not need SQL queries over those files. Which Azure service should be considered first?

options.text: Azure Files, because it provides a managed file share that can look like a mounted folder.
Azure SQL Database, because a folder path can be represented as a table column.
Cosmos DB, because files can be converted to JSON documents.
A managed disk attached to every worker at the same time, because disks are always shareable.

questions.explanation: Azure Files fits the shared-folder shape. Managed disks are attached to VMs, while SQL and Cosmos DB are data services for different access patterns.

questions.scenario: A VM-based worker needs a durable boot and data volume. The data does not need to be shared by several machines at the same time. Which Azure storage shape is the closer fit?

options.text: A managed disk attached to the VM.
A Blob Storage container mounted as the operating system disk.
A Cosmos DB container with partition key /disk.
An Azure SQL database with one row per disk block.

questions.explanation: Managed disks are block-level volumes for VM-shaped workloads. They are the right mental model when the operating system expects a disk.
